temples.name: ISKCON Delhi
temples.address: Sant Nagar, East of Kailash, New Delhi

temples.name: ISKCON Bangalore
temples.address: Hare Krishna Hill, Rajaji Nagar, Bangalore

menu.name: Maha Prasadam Thali
menu.description: Complete thali with 2 sabzis, dal, rice, roti, raita, papad, and kheer — offered to Lord Krishna

menu.name: Ekadashi Thali
menu.description: Special fasting thali with sabudana khichdi, sendha namak preparations, fruits and falahaar

menu.name: Janmashtami Thali
menu.description: Festive thali prepared on auspicious occasions with panchamrit and special bhog items

menu.name: Satvik Dal Rice
menu.description: Simple pure dal chawal with ghee, cooked without onion-garlic in the temple tradition

menu.name: Paneer Sabzi & Roti
menu.description: Fresh paneer cooked in tomato-based masala, served with soft whole wheat rotis

menu.name: Khichdi Prasadam
menu.description: The sacred khichdi of Jagannath, cooked in earthen pots with pure ghee and temple spices

menu.name: Sabudana Khichdi
menu.description: Light and nutritious sago pearls with peanuts, cooked in pure ghee — perfect for Ekadashi

menu.name: Makhana Kheer
menu.description: Fox nut kheer slow-cooked in full cream milk with cardamom, saffron and dry fruits

menu.name: Aloo Sendha Namak
menu.description: Satvik potatoes cooked in rock salt and pure ghee, a staple Ekadashi preparation

menu.name: Ladoo Prasadam
menu.description: The legendary besan ladoos of ISKCON, offered to Radha-Krishna and then distributed as prasadam

menu.name: Panchamrit
menu.description: Sacred mixture of milk, curd, ghee, honey, and sugar — used in abhishek rituals

menu.name: Mathura Peda
menu.description: Soft milk-based sweets from the land of Krishna, made with pure khoya and cardamom

menu.name: Janmashtami Bhog
menu.description: 56 item bhog prepared on the auspicious birthday of Lord Krishna — a divine feast

menu.name: Holi Special Thandai
menu.description: Traditional chilled thandai with almonds, rose petals, cardamom and milk

menu.name: Diwali Mithai Box
menu.description: Assorted temple sweets — kaju katli, barfi, besan ladoo packed in an auspicious gift box

donations.name: Annadaan - Feed the Needy
donations.description: Sponsor a complete meal for someone in need. ₹50 feeds one person.

donations.name: Temple Kitchen Support
donations.description: Help maintain and upgrade ISKCON temple kitchens across India.

donations.name: Go-Seva (Cow Protection)
donations.description: Support temple cows that provide pure milk for prasadam preparations.
